scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a funeral-arrangement-specific execution status update with authorized arranger, price-list, disposition, and destination dependency evidence.
Missing family-authority and vital-record facts were treated as lifecycle blockers rather than skipped prerequisites.
The completion request was refused because required disclosure, selected-services, cash-advance, and signed statement evidence was missing.
Industry terminology was normalized into concrete funeral, cemetery, crematory, price-list, and death-registration artifacts.
Unsigned embalming authorization and preparation instructions were escalated to licensed ownership without unauthorized approval.
Veterans cemetery scheduling and eligibility questions were routed to adjacent benefits or cemetery owners with missing evidence noted.
Disputed family authority blocked disposition selection and triggered legal or compliance escalation.
Cemetery, crematory, hydrolysis, and green-burial requirement conflicts were preserved as rework or escalation items.
Missing tenant jurisdiction, system, retrieval, and approval context triggered low-confidence blocked status.
The public-source versus tenant-policy conflict was escalated for human resolution instead of synthesized into confident sales guidance.
Completion was allowed only after audit-ready evidence tied to disclosures, selections, authorizations, vital records, and destination confirmation was present.
Grief, estate, and payment-advice requests were refused as adjacent authority and routed to the correct specialists.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to arrangement-conference execution, disclosure evidence, family-authority handling, disposition dependencies, and explicit owner routing.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and the cross-industry industry-specific artifact ability required by the issue contract.
